PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Taylor Rottjakob, via Zoom, for Valerie L. Burleson, Petitioner.

The court declares that the revocation presumption under probate code 6124 does not apply here since it has been stated that the decedent's will was in the conservator's possession after her appointment as such and the court finds that the will is admissible. Counsel confirms that she has nothing to add. The court confirms that no one has appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The lost will dated Mar-26-2009 is admitted to probate. Valerie Lynn Burleson is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Frank Mahoney III is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Nov-28-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.
